speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Kill the poys and the luggage! ’Tis expressly

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: against the law of arms. ’Tis as arrant a piece of

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: knavery, mark you now, as can be offert, in your

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: conscience now, is it not?

speaker: GOWER
text: ’Tis certain there’s not a boy left alive, and

speaker: GOWER
text: the cowardly rascals that ran from the battle ha’

speaker: GOWER
text: done this slaughter. Besides, they have burned

speaker: GOWER
text: and carried away all that was in the King’s tent,

speaker: GOWER
text: wherefore the King, most worthily, hath caused

speaker: GOWER
text: every soldier to cut his prisoner’s throat. O, ’tis a

speaker: GOWER
text: gallant king!

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Ay, he was porn at Monmouth, Captain

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Gower. What call you the town’s name where

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Alexander the Pig was born?

speaker: GOWER
text: Alexander the Great.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Why, I pray you, is not “pig” great? The pig,

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: or the great, or the mighty, or the huge, or the

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: magnanimous, are all one reckonings, save the

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: phrase is a little variations.

speaker: GOWER
text: I think Alexander the Great was born in Macedon.

speaker: GOWER
text: His father was called Philip of Macedon, as I

speaker: GOWER
text: take it.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: I think it is in Macedon where Alexander is

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: porn. I tell you, captain, if you look in the maps of

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: the ’orld, I warrant you sall find, in the comparisons

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: between Macedon and Monmouth, that the

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: situations, look you, is both alike. There is a river in

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Macedon, and there is also, moreover, a river at

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Monmouth. It is called Wye at Monmouth, but it is

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: out of my prains what is the name of the other river.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: But ’tis all one; ’tis alike as my fingers is to my

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: fingers, and there is salmons in both. If you mark

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Alexander’s life well, Harry of Monmouth’s life is

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: come after it indifferent well, for there is figures in

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: all things. Alexander, God knows and you know, in

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: his rages and his furies and his wraths and his

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: cholers and his moods and his displeasures and his

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: indignations, and also being a little intoxicates in

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: his prains, did, in his ales and his angers, look you,

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: kill his best friend, Cleitus.

speaker: GOWER
text: Our king is not like him in that. He never

speaker: GOWER
text: killed any of his friends.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: It is not well done, mark you now, to take

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: the tales out of my mouth ere it is made and

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: finished. I speak but in the figures and comparisons

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: of it. As Alexander killed his friend Cleitus, being in

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: his ales and his cups, so also Harry Monmouth,

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: being in his right wits and his good judgments,

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: turned away the fat knight with the great-belly

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: doublet; he was full of jests and gipes and knaveries

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: and mocks—I have forgot his name.

speaker: GOWER
text: Sir John Falstaff.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: That is he. I’ll tell you, there is good men

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: porn at Monmouth.

speaker: GOWER
text: Here comes his Majesty.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I was not angry since I came to France

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Until this instant. Take a trumpet, herald.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ride thou unto the horsemen on yond hill.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If they will fight with us, bid them come down,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Or void the field. They do offend our sight.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If they’ll do neither, we will come to them

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And make them skirr away as swift as stones

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Enforcèd from the old Assyrian slings.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Besides, we’ll cut the throats of those we have,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And not a man of them that we shall take

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Shall taste our mercy. Go and tell them so.

speaker: EXETER
text: Here comes the herald of the French, my liege.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: His eyes are humbler than they used to be.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How now, what means this, herald? Know’st thou not

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That I have fined these bones of mine for ransom?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Com’st thou again for ransom?

speaker: MONTJOY
text: No, great king.

speaker: MONTJOY
text: I come to thee for charitable license,

speaker: MONTJOY
text: That we may wander o’er this bloody field

speaker: MONTJOY
text: To book our dead and then to bury them,

speaker: MONTJOY
text: To sort our nobles from our common men,

speaker: MONTJOY
text: For many of our princes—woe the while!—

speaker: MONTJOY
text: Lie drowned and soaked in mercenary blood.

speaker: MONTJOY
text: So do our vulgar drench their peasant limbs

speaker: MONTJOY
text: In blood of princes, and the wounded steeds

speaker: MONTJOY
text: Fret fetlock deep in gore, and with wild rage

speaker: MONTJOY
text: Yerk out their armèd heels at their dead masters,

speaker: MONTJOY
text: Killing them twice. O, give us leave, great king,

speaker: MONTJOY
text: To view the field in safety and dispose

speaker: MONTJOY
text: Of their dead bodies.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I tell thee truly, herald,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I know not if the day be ours or no,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For yet a many of your horsemen peer

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And gallop o’er the field.

speaker: MONTJOY
text: The day is yours.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Praised be God, and not our strength, for it!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What is this castle called that stands hard by?

speaker: MONTJOY
text: They call it Agincourt.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Then call we this the field of Agincourt,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Fought on the day of Crispin Crispianus.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Your grandfather of famous memory, an ’t

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: please your Majesty, and your great-uncle Edward

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: the Plack Prince of Wales, as I have read in the

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: chronicles, fought a most prave pattle here in

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: France.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: They did, Fluellen.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Your Majesty says very true. If your Majesties

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: is remembered of it, the Welshmen did good

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: service in a garden where leeks did grow, wearing

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: leeks in their Monmouth caps, which, your Majesty

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: know, to this hour is an honorable badge of the

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: service. And I do believe your Majesty takes no

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: scorn to wear the leek upon Saint Tavy’s day.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I wear it for a memorable honor,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For I am Welsh, you know, good countryman.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: All the water in Wye cannot wash your

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Majesty’s Welsh plood out of your pody, I can tell

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: you that. God pless it and preserve it as long as it

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: pleases his Grace and his Majesty too.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thanks, good my countryman.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: By Jeshu, I am your Majesty’s countryman,

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: I care not who know it. I will confess it to all the

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: ’orld. I need not to be ashamed of your Majesty,

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: praised be God, so long as your Majesty is an

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: honest man.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: God keep me so.—Our heralds, go with him.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Bring me just notice of the numbers dead

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: On both our parts.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Call yonder fellow hither.

speaker: EXETER
text: Soldier, you must come to the King.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Soldier, why wear’st thou that glove in thy

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: cap?

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: An ’t please your Majesty, ’tis the gage of

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: one that I should fight withal, if he be alive.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: An Englishman?

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: An ’t please your Majesty, a rascal that

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: swaggered with me last night, who, if alive and ever

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: dare to challenge this glove, I have sworn to take

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: him a box o’ th’ ear, or if I can see my glove in his

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: cap, which he swore, as he was a soldier, he would

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: wear if alive, I will strike it out soundly.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What think you, Captain Fluellen, is it fit

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: this soldier keep his oath?

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: He is a craven and a villain else, an ’t

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: please your Majesty, in my conscience.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: It may be his enemy is a gentleman of

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: great sort, quite from the answer of his degree.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Though he be as good a gentleman as the

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: devil is, as Lucifer and Beelzebub himself, it is

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: necessary, look your Grace, that he keep his vow

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: and his oath. If he be perjured, see you now, his

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: reputation is as arrant a villain and a Jack Sauce as

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: ever his black shoe trod upon God’s ground and His

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: earth, in my conscience, la.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Then keep thy vow, sirrah, when thou

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: meet’st the fellow.

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: So I will, my liege, as I live.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Who serv’st thou under?

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: Under Captain Gower, my liege.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Gower is a good captain, and is good knowledge

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: and literatured in the wars.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Call him hither to me, soldier.

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: I will, my liege.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Here,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Fluellen, wear thou this favor for me, and stick it in

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: thy cap. When Alençon and myself were down

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: together, I plucked this glove from his helm. If any

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: man challenge this, he is a friend to Alençon and an

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: enemy to our person. If thou encounter any such,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: apprehend him, an thou dost me love.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Your Grace

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: does me as great honors as can be desired in the

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: hearts of his subjects. I would fain see the man that

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: has but two legs that shall find himself aggriefed at

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: this glove, that is all; but I would fain see it once, an

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: please God of His grace that I might see.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Know’st thou Gower?

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: He is my dear friend, an please you.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Pray thee, go seek him, and bring him to

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: my tent.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: I will fetch him.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My Lord of Warwick and my brother Gloucester,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Follow Fluellen closely at the heels.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The glove which I have given him for a favor

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: May haply purchase him a box o’ th’ ear.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: It is the soldier’s. I by bargain should

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Wear it myself. Follow, good cousin Warwick.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If that the soldier strike him, as I judge

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: By his blunt bearing he will keep his word,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Some sudden mischief may arise of it,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For I do know Fluellen valiant

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And, touched with choler, hot as gunpowder,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And quickly will return an injury.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Follow, and see there be no harm between them.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Go you with me, uncle of Exeter.
